ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Confidential, non-binding Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Helion Care Insights, Inc. (“Helion”) by Norvera Health Bancorp, Inc. (“Norvera”), a U.S. reporting issuer listed on a U.S. national securities exchange.
Structure and consideration: All-cash reverse triangular merger at $8.50 per Helion share; estimated equity value ~$212,000,000; subject to customary working-capital and debt-like adjustments.
Financing: Cash on hand and a committed bridge facility from Redfield Commercial Bank, N.A. (commitment letter executed and deliverable at signing); no financing condition to close.

Materiality standard (issuer-relative): For purposes of Rule 10b‑5 and internal disclosure controls, items reasonably expected to impact >5% of Norvera’s consolidated revenue or quarterly EPS, or to alter previously issued guidance, are presumptively material; immaterial operational details may be shared internally on a need-to-know basis.

Governance and approvals: Subject to approval by both Boards of Directors and receipt of required healthcare, insurance, and banking regulatory notices/clearances; pre-merger antitrust notifications under applicable U.S. law will be filed promptly after signing.

Definitive agreement conditions: Confirmatory diligence; absence of a Company Material Adverse Effect; customary representations and warranties; covenants re interim operations; closing targeted within 90–120 days post-signing.

No-shop and fiduciary out: Helion will agree to a no-shop with customary fiduciary out and matching rights; termination fee equal to 2.5% of equity value payable upon acceptance of a Superior Proposal or willful breach.

Non-issuer operational note (benign): Helion’s planned office relocation in August 2026 is a landlord matter and, standing alone, is not expected to be material to Norvera or to alter previously issued guidance.
quarterly blackout from June 15 through July 25
Only essential personnel will be wall-crossed and tracked
no reference to this term sheet prior to public dissemination
no one-on-one calls with research analysts prior to announcement
shall not trade in Norvera or Helion securities

Confidential, non-binding Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Helion Care Insights, Inc. (“Helion”) by Norvera Health Bancorp, Inc. (“Norvera”), a U.S. reporting issuer listed on a U.S. national securities exchange.
Structure and consideration: All-cash reverse triangular merger at $8.50 per Helion share; estimated equity value ~$212,000,000; subject to customary working-capital and debt-like adjustments.
Financing: Cash on hand and a committed bridge facility from Redfield Commercial Bank, N.A. (commitment letter executed and deliverable at signing); no financing condition to close.
Public disclosure and timing: Public announcement by broadly disseminated press release and investor webcast prior to market open on the first trading day after signing; Form 8‑K (Item 1.01) to be filed within four business days of definitive agreement; no reference to this term sheet prior to public dissemination.
MNPI/Reg FD: Until public dissemination as described above, all terms are material non-public information; selective disclosure to covered market participants (e.g., sell-side analysts, broker-dealers, or institutional investors) is prohibited under Regulation FD; if any non-intentional selective disclosure occurs, the disclosing party will promptly cure via a broadly disseminated press release.
Blackout/trading restrictions: Norvera is in a quarterly blackout from June 15 through July 25 pending Q2 results; directors, officers, and wall-crossed employees shall not trade in Norvera or Helion securities until after public announcement and lifting of internal restrictions.
Public-status reference: As previously announced in Norvera’s April 30, 2026 press release and accompanying Form 8‑K, the Board commenced a review of strategic alternatives; this proposed transaction is a potential outcome of that publicly disclosed process.
Healthcare and banking data handling: Pre-signing diligence will use de-identified, aggregated data only; no Protected Health Information under HIPAA or Nonpublic Personal Information under GLBA will be shared pre-signing except in a monitored clean room with access logs and return-or-destroy protocols.
Materiality standard (issuer-relative): For purposes of Rule 10b‑5 and internal disclosure controls, items reasonably expected to impact >5% of Norvera’s consolidated revenue or quarterly EPS, or to alter previously issued guidance, are presumptively material; immaterial operational details may be shared internally on a need-to-know basis.
Governance and approvals: Subject to approval by both Boards of Directors and receipt of required healthcare, insurance, and banking regulatory notices/clearances; pre-merger antitrust notifications under applicable U.S. law will be filed promptly after signing.
Definitive agreement conditions: Confirmatory diligence; absence of a Company Material Adverse Effect; customary representations and warranties; covenants re interim operations; closing targeted within 90–120 days post-signing.
No-shop and fiduciary out: Helion will agree to a no-shop with customary fiduciary out and matching rights; termination fee equal to 2.5% of equity value payable upon acceptance of a Superior Proposal or willful breach.
Employee and analyst workflows: Only essential personnel will be wall-crossed and tracked; no one-on-one calls with research analysts prior to announcement; any inquiries will be routed to Norvera Investor Relations at ir@norverahb.com.
Stale public information (non-MNPI): Helion’s Form 10‑K for the year ended December 31, 2024 (filed March 1, 2025) disclosed segment revenue detail and reported no material weaknesses in internal control; these disclosures are already public and not restricted by this term sheet.
Non-issuer operational note (benign): Helion’s planned office relocation in August 2026 is a landlord matter and, standing alone, is not expected to be material to Norvera or to alter previously issued guidance.
Governing law and enforceability: Delaware law governs; this term sheet is non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity (45 days), expense reimbursement, and governing law; each party makes customary 10b‑5 compliance acknowledgments regarding truthfulness and completeness of shared information.
Contacts: Ms. Liora Mendel, General Counsel, Norvera (liora.mendel@norverahb.com), and Mr. Garret Osei, SVP, Redfield Commercial Bank, N.A. (gosei@redfieldcb.com); de
